RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 135-AA, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Wick Thomas, a Democrat, proudly represents part of Jackson County (District 19) in the Missouri House of Representatives. They were elected to their first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Thomas is a lifelong Missourian, growing up in Drexel. They attended the University of Missouri-Kansas City, where they earned a Bachelor of Arts in Communications, and later obtained a Master's degree in Library Sciences from Emporia State University. Most recently a Faculty Librarian for Metropolitan Community College – Kansas City, Rep. Thomas has also worked for the Kansas City Public Library as a Teen Services Librarian, and the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art as a Manager of Teen Programs, bringing a wealth of experience in education, public service, and the arts to their legislative work.
Outside of their legislative duties, Rep. Thomas regularly volunteers for nonprofits within their district, helps build and fill little free libraries, and has received recognition for their activism and leadership. They were named a "Mover and Shaker" by the Library Journal, awarded Best Activist by Pitch Magazine, and honored with the Creating Change Award from The National LGBTQ Taskforce.
Rep. Thomas is happily married to their spouse, Clay, with whom they share a small historic arts business and work on their historic home and urban homestead. Their career is marked by a deep commitment to community service, education, and social justice.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to higher education tuition
Repeals the state prohibition of local governments regulating paper and plastic bags

Requires school districts to provide "period products" at no cost in middle schools and high schools and charter schools and repeals provisions prohibiting abortion services providers from providing instruction on human sexuality or sexually transmitted diseases
Modifies the required school year start date for school districts in which a charter school operates
Extends the expiration date of a provision allowing retired teachers to serve as substitute teachers without a discontinuance of such teacher's retirement allowance
Abolishes the death penalty and specifies that any person sentenced to death must be sentenced to life imprisonment without parole
Repeals provisions that prohibit political subdivisions from adopting orders, ordinances, or regulations relating to firearms
Repeals the prohibition on municipal sanctuary policies
Requires the secretary of state to provide all printed materials in Spanish, upon request
Repeals provisions relating to gender transition procedures
Repeals provisions prohibiting same sex marriages
Prohibits discrimination based on sexual orientation, gender identity, or veteran status
Exempts entities registered with the department of health and senior services that distribute hypodermic needles or syringes from the crime of unlawful delivery of drug paraphernalia
Urges for the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment
Proposes a constitutional amendment providing that marriage exists only between individuals who are 18 years of age or older

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies when a registration-inspection certificate can be granted based on the grantees handling of certain nonnative invasive plant species
Bans publicly funded libraries from banning books
Establishes the "Media Literacy and Critical Thinking Act"
Abolishes the death penalty and specifies that any person sentenced to death must be sentenced to life imprisonment without parole
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Requires in-state public educational institutions to grant undergraduate course credit for students who score 4 or higher on international baccalaureate examinations
Requires in-state public educational institutions to grant undergraduate course credit for students who score 4 or higher on international baccalaureate examinations
Prohibits certain mental health professionals from engaging in conversion therapy with minors
Requires members of the general assembly to stay present in a hearing when a bill they sponsor is being heard
Prioritizes the use of organized labor for state contracts in certain industries
Allows for 3 weeks of no-excuse absentee voting
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment establishing the right to make medical decisions, including decisions on gender-affirming care
Proposes a constitutional amendment prohibiting an initiative measure approved by the voters to be amended or repealed by the General Assembly except by referendum
